vibe_report.summary: A refined, low-contrast monochrome palette layered with warm wood and brass accents. The overall direction is meditative and textural: matte black walls and smoked oak slatted panels anchor low-profile furniture, amber cove and recessed strip lights provide warm contrast to exterior daylight, and sparse sculptural objects (bonsai, ceramic vessels, a single bookshelf) give the scenes a calm, curated feel. Maintain architectural fidelity, keep verticals true, and derive all light from visible windows and practical fixtures.
vibe_report.palette_rationale: A base of deep charcoals and near-black (anchors and negative space) balanced by mid-tone warm wood (smoked oak/walnut) and restrained brass highlights for small accents. A pale neutral linen/stone acts only as occasional contrast to prevent loss of detail in shadowed areas.
vibe_report.lighting_mood: Dramatic, directional, editorial. Primary illumination comes from exterior windows (daylight) with controlled warm supplement from amber cove/strip LEDs and a few sculptural brass lamp practicals. Shadows should be crisp and sculptural; avoid flat or evenly lit interiors.
materials: warm brass fixtures and lamp bases
textures: soft wool rugs in muted charcoal tones
furniture_language: solid slab coffee tables (monolithic black stone)
minimal open bookshelves with curated volumes
slab dining table with slender brass/wood pendant
integrated built-ins with vertical slat faces
styling_rules: Keep furnishings low and horizontal to emphasize calm planes.
Limit palette to five primary neutrals/accents; no saturated color pops beyond subtle brass or wood grain.
Curate accessories: one bonsai or sculptural object per vignette, a small stack of books, one ceramic vessel.
Use warm amber strip lighting behind slats or headboards — always visible as a practical source.
Preserve visible light-source logic: every warm accent must be explained by a visible fixture or recessed strip.
avoid: added windows or light sources not on the floor plan

room_guidance.headline: Japandi Noir Living — low, meditative, & sculptural
room_guidance.guidance: Stage a low charcoal linen sectional centered on a large muted rug facing the bay/exterior glazing. Add a monolithic black stone coffee table with a single bonsai and one stack of books. Smoked oak vertical slat panel on the main wall with recessed amber cove behind the slats. Use frosted shoji panel as a soft partition (if plan allows) to frame light without adding new openings. Include a recessed floor lamp or brass table lamp as a visible warm practical.
must_include: smoked oak vertical slat wall with amber cove

room_guidance.headline: Primary Bedroom — platform calm with strip light drama
room_guidance.guidance: Low walnut platform bed with charcoal linens and textured throws. Fluted headboard panel or vertical slats behind the bed with a recessed warm LED strip that reads as the practical light source. Brass dome bedside lamp on one side only for asymmetry. Keep bedside surfaces minimal: one ceramic tray and one book.
must_include: amber recessed LED strip behind headboard
must_avoid: excess bedside lamps or pendant clusters

room_guidance.headline: Minimal dark kitchen — tactile surfaces, warm accents
room_guidance.guidance: Matte black cabinet faces with warm wood open shelving and a narrow, integrated bookshelf niche for cookbooks. Countertops in honed dark stone. Add a single pendant in satin brass above the sink or prep area and under-cabinet warm strip light to create practical amber fills. Keep styling minimal: one ceramic pitcher, a small stack of plates, and 3–4 curated books on the shelf.

room_guidance.headline: Bay dining vignette — intimate, warm, and sculptural
room_guidance.guidance: A low-profile rectangular dining table in smoked oak with slender dark chairs. Pendant cluster: one low satin-brass pendant centered above the table (visible practical). Bay windows allow directional daylight; add amber strip recessed in the bay seat or ceiling reveal for evening mood. Keep tabletop minimal: a single fluted ceramic vessel or a small stack of books.
must_include: bay window seating or reveal with amber strip

room_guidance.headline: Guest/secondary bedroom — refined restraint
room_guidance.guidance: Smaller platform bed with charcoal linens, a narrow bookshelf against the wall (to meet 'bookshelf' requirement), and a single sculptural lamp. Vertical slat detail as a partial headboard is optional. Keep accessories sparse to preserve calm.

room_guidance.headline: Compact restful room — textured and minimal
room_guidance.guidance: Simple low bed or daybed with charcoal linen, a single wall-mounted reading lamp (brass), and a small open shelf for books. Use a narrow smoked-oak slatted accent to tie back to main palette. Keep circulation clear — minimal furniture.

warnings: Natural light varies by room; do not introduce additional window openings in staging or video lighting.
Preserve exact room geometry and measured furniture footprints to avoid scale drift in video.
Avoid glossy reflective materials that will create specular hotspots against matte finishes.

overall_mood: Moody, calm, textural luxury — cinematic Japandi noir with warm amber accents
global_style_notes: Preserve all architectural lines and measured room geometry; correct perspective but do not alter layout.
Image-pass recommended for all rooms to achieve editorial, directional light and maintain vertical integrity.
Derive all lighting behavior from visible windows and practicals (pendants, recessed strips, lamps).
Keep camera motion slow and deliberate to match the meditative vibe; prefer low-angle framing for horizontal emphasis.
negative_prompt: blur, distort, low quality, warped architecture, flicker, unstable camera, unrealistic lighting, muddy shadows, layout drift, added windows

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Run one editorial image-pass to introduce crisp directional shadows from bay glazing and to deepen matte black wall textures. Preserve the existing exterior bay window as the primary daylight source; supplement with visible amber cove behind the smoked-oak slat wall and one sculptural brass floor lamp as a practical. Keep exposure balanced so deep shadows retain texture (do not clip blacks).
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Wide living room composition, camera placed at ~40" (low eye level) to emphasize horizontal planes. Layout: low charcoal linen sectional centered facing exterior bay glazing (north), monolithic black stone coffee table with single bonsai and a slim stack of books, smoked-oak vertical slat feature wall with recessed amber cove behind slats on the main interior wall. Frosted shoji panel as a soft partition at one side (optional). Materials: matte charcoal plaster walls, microcement floor, smoked oak slats, honed black stone table, brass floor lamp with low-intensity warm filament visible. Visible light sources: bay exterior glazing (directional daylight), amber recessed strip behind slats (practical), floor lamp. Maintain true verticals and realistic furniture scale for a 12.17' x 14.67' room. Styling: sparse bookshelf against a side wall with curated volumes and one ceramic vessel. Camera should not introduce new architectural elements; do not add skylights or extra windows.
room_plans.video_prompt: Very slow dolly in, camera moves forward from the rear of the living area toward the bay glazing, directional daylight from bay windows shifts across matte walls while warm amber cove and a floor lamp provide steady warm fill, gradual shadow crawl across furniture, consistent exposure, stable motion, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: bay exterior glazing with directional daylight
smoked-oak vertical slat wall with amber cove

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Perform a single editorial image-pass to refine headboard slat geometry, increase sculptural shadow definition from window light, and dial amber LED strip warmness so it reads as a visible practical. Keep overall exposure slightly lowered to preserve mood but lift midtones for texture legibility.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Bedroom framed from a corner near the doorway at ~36" height to show platform bed and headboard slat wall. Layout: low walnut platform bed (centered on longest wall) with charcoal linens and textured throw; vertical slatted headboard/fluted panel with a recessed amber LED strip behind it. One satin-brass dome bedside lamp on the left side (visible practical). Rug under bed sized proportionally for a 11.33' x 13.75' room. Materials: matte charcoal plaster, walnut bed frame, charcoal linen, warm LED strip. Visible light sources: exterior window (bottom wall) and headboard recessed strip and bedside lamp. Maintain measured scale; avoid adding multiple lamps or extra bright overheads.
room_plans.video_prompt: Slow static zoom in on bed area with subtle parallax, camera eases closer to reveal headboard texture and amber strip light, daylight from window provides directional fill while recessed strip and bedside lamp give warm contrast, exposure stable, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: low walnut platform bed with charcoal linens
vertical slat/fluted headboard with amber recessed LED

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Run an editorial image-pass focused on material separation: preserve matte black cabinetry and reveal wood grain on open shelving. Add under-cabinet warm strip lighting as the visible practical; avoid global brightening. Keep the countertop texture legible by balancing shadows from a single pendant practical.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Kitchen mid-shot showing run of matte black cabinetry on one wall and open smoked-oak shelving with curated books and ceramics. Include a narrow integrated bookshelf niche for cookbooks. Countertop in honed dark stone, subtle under-cabinet warm LED strip visible. One satin-brass pendant over prep area/peninsula (if plan allows placement). Visible light sources: small window toward dining side (directional daylight), under-cabinet strips, pendant. Maintain realistic scale for a 9.5' x 10.58' kitchen; do not stage island larger than the room allows. Keep appliances minimal and matte-black to avoid specular highlights.
room_plans.video_prompt: Slow truck right, camera glides laterally along the kitchen run revealing smoked-oak shelving and under-cabinet warm strips, directional daylight from the dining side window shifts across textured surfaces, exposure steady, cinematic, photorealistic
must_include: smoked-oak open shelving with curated books

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Apply one editorial image-pass to define bay-window geometry and to emphasize the pendant as the visible practical. Accent the bay seat or ceiling reveal with a subtle amber strip for evening mood. Keep exterior bay glazing as primary daylight; balance the pendant so it doesn't clip highlights.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Dining scene oriented toward the bay window (right-side elevation) shot from a low three-quarter angle to capture table and bay. Layout: low-profile smoked-oak dining table sized for the 9.33' x 12' room, slender dark chairs, single satin-brass pendant centered. Bay window seating or reveal with recessed amber strip visible. Minimal tabletop styling: one fluted ceramic vessel. Materials: smoked oak, matte plaster, warm brass pendant. Visible light sources: bay glazing, brass pendant, amber strip in bay reveal. Preserve verticals and window geometry; do not add extra glazing.
room_plans.video_prompt: Parallax orbit, super smooth camera travels in a gentle arc around the dining table while keeping the table centered, daylight pours in from the bay windows and amber pendant reads as a warm practical, shadows shift slowly, consistent exposure, cinematic, photorealistic

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Do an image-pass to tighten perspective and to boost headboard and bookshelf texture. Use a single wall-mounted or table sculptural lamp as the visible practical; ensure the bookshelf reads as genuine with real book spines and no overly colorful covers.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Secondary bedroom with low platform bed (charcoal linens) shot from doorway level to show bed and narrow bookshelf on a side wall. Include a narrow open bookshelf with curated, muted-spine volumes to satisfy the objects list. One sculptural brass lamp (table or wall-mounted) provides warm fill; exterior window on right elevation provides directional daylight. Materials: matte plaster, walnut/ smoked oak accents, charcoal textiles. Maintain believable scale for an 11.25' x 11.33' room and keep verticals true.
room_plans.video_prompt: Slow dolly in, camera moves toward the bed and bookshelf area, daylight from side window provides directional modeling while a sculptural brass lamp offers warm practical contrast, exposure steady, cinematic, photorealistic

room_plans.lighting_instruction: Single image-pass to correct perspective and to increase textural separation. Use a brass wall-mounted reading lamp as the visible practical and a small slatted accent to connect to the main material language. Keep exposure slightly lower to retain mood.
room_plans.dalle_scene_details: Compact bedroom/daybed vignette shot from a corner to show the bed/daybed and small open shelf for books. Low bed with charcoal linens, brass wall-mounted reading lamp visible next to bed, smoked-oak slat accent on the wall. Visible light sources: left-elevation window and wall-mounted lamp. Maintain realistic furniture scale for a 10' x 10' room, and ensure all vertical lines are true and orthogonal.
room_plans.video_prompt: Static slow zoom in on daybed area, camera eases closer to reveal texture of linens and slat detail while daylight from left window provides soft directional fill, warm lamp remains steady as a practical, exposure stable, cinematic, photorealistic

warnings: All rooms recommended for an editorial image-pass before video to ensure stable verticals, crisp directional light, and filmic contrast.
Preserve exact architecture and measured furniture footprints—do not change window positions or room geometry in image or video passes.
Suppress any generated effects that introduce impossible lighting (e.g., light coming from non-visible walls or floating sources).
